   (0.49 + 0.21) s – 1.05 = 9.45

Question: Emiliano thinks $$x = 40$$ is the solution to the equation $$\frac{1}{2} x = 80$$ . Explain why he is wrong.
o3 Answer:
Emiliano has misunderstood what the equation ½ x = 80 means.  

• ½ x means “one‐half of x.”  
• If x were 40, one‐half of 40 is 20, not 80:  
 ½ · 40 = 20 ≠ 80.

To find the correct value of x, multiply both sides of the equation by 2 (the reciprocal of ½):

 ½ x = 80  
 2 · ½ x = 2 · 80  
 x = 160.

So the solution is x = 160, not 40.

(½)·40 = 20,  
not 80. Hence the correct solution is x = 160.
